figure.title: Accelerator Key Distribution Topology
figure.purpose: Illustrates how encryption keys are assigned and distributed across different ports of multiple accelerators in a switched topology.

technical_summary: Shows four Accelerators (0, 1, 2, 3) connected via two Switches (0, 1).
Text overlays indicate specific key assignments for TX and RX ports.
For example, Accelerator 0 Port 0 TX has keys K001, K002, K003. Port 1 TX has K011, K012, K013.
Receiving ports (e.g., Accelerator 1 Port 0 RX) have corresponding keys (K001, K201, K301) to decrypt traffic from the respective source transmitters.

critical_facts: Keys are assigned per-port and per-path, ensuring that specific communication channels between accelerators are uniquely secured.
